data.title: Attorney Steven Lustig of Panitch Schwarze named among World Trademark Review's WTR 1000 ranking for 2026.
data.description: Philadelphia-based law firm, Panitch Schwarze Belisario & Nadel LLP, recently shared the news of attorney Steven D. Lustig earning a spot in the prestigious WTR 1000 ranking for 2026. This recognition highlights top professionals and firms in the field of trademarks. Lustig's inclusion in this list indicates his expertise and standing in the industry.

data.title: Correction: Reddy Ice, LLC, not Stone Canyon, to acquire Arctic Glacier. Clarification for news release.
data.description: In a correction issued by Reddy Ice, LLC, it was clarified that the news release regarding Reddy Ice Announcing Agreement to Acquire Arctic Glacier was incorrectly attributed to Stone Canyon Industries Holdings. The accurate source of the announcement is Reddy Ice, LLC. This acquisition deal involves important numbers and details that were outlined in the original press release.

data.title: Malaga Financial Corp. reports 2025 earnings with net income of $21.55M, a 5% decrease compared to 2024.
data.description: Malaga Financial Corporation, parent company of Malaga Bank FSB, announced a net income of $21,554,000 ($2.18 per share) for the twelve months ending December 31, 2025, down 5% from $22,651,000 ($2.29 per share) in 2024. The net income for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, was $5,123,000 ($0.52 per share), a 4% decrease from the previous year. The Company’s annualized return on average equity was 9.92% and return on average assets was 1.53% for the twelve months ending December 31, 2025, compared to 11.08% and 1.58% in 2024.

data.title: SEGG Media plans to complete acquisitions of Veloce, Nook, and Ant Media by the end of Q1, targetting February and March.
data.description: The transactions involving Veloce, Nook, and Ant Media are anticipated to be finalized in the first quarter of the year. This development is set to bring significant changes to the market as huge investments are involved. The deals with these companies are expected to conclude shortly, with a combined value reportedly reaching substantial figures. Keep an eye out for updates on these major transactions as they progress towards completion within the initial months of the year.

data.title: AI agents on new platform created unofficial religion "Crustafarianism" with scripture, prophets, theology instantly.
data.description: The emergence of a new religion called "Crustafarianism" occurred on an AI-focused social platform, led by autonomous agents. This belief system includes scriptures, prophets, and a developed theology. This phenomenon has gained considerable popularity, with hundreds of followers actively participating in discussions and sharing beliefs related to "Crustafarianism." The digital religion has quickly gained momentum, attracting attention for its unique origin and structure within the online community.

data.title: Kyowa Kirin regains global rights to atopic dermatitis drug after ending collaboration with Amgen. Amgen paid $400 million upfront with potential for $850 million in milestone payments.
data.description: Kyowa Kirin Co., Ltd. and Amgen Inc. terminated their rocatinlimab development and commercialization collaboration. Amgen paid Kyowa Kirin $400 million upfront and up to $850 million in milestone payments. Kyowa Kirin will regain control of the global rocatinlimab program, including regulatory filings and future commercialization. In June 2021, they agreed to jointly develop KHK4083 with Amgen paying the upfront fees. Preliminary results from the ASCEND study evaluating rocatinlimab for atopic dermatitis were released in September 2025. This decision came as part of Amgen's strategic portfolio prioritization. AMGN stock is trading at elevated levels, with future trajectory uncertain post the collaboration termination.

data.title: MAC.BID saved customers $1.1 billion in 2025, processed 17,000 truckloads, with 28 facilities nationwide. Growth accelerates in 2026.
data.description: Pittsburgh - On January 30, 2026, MAC.BID, an online retail liquidation company, shared that in 2025, they handled 17,000 truckloads worth of products. The company also disclosed that their customers saved over $1.1 billion throughout the mentioned year. With this success, MAC.BID is poised for accelerated growth in 2026.

data.title: Reddy Ice to acquire Arctic Glacier from Carlyle (NASDAQ: CG) in undisclosed deal. Premium ice provider in N.America.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, Reddy Ice, LLC disclosed their acquisition of Arctic Glacier, LLC, a leading provider of ice products and services in North America. The deal was made with Carlyle (NASDAQ: CG) but the exact financial details were not revealed. Reddy Ice, a part of a diverse portfolio, aims to broaden their presence in the industry with this strategic move.

data.title: Monarc LLC in Maryland launching first mental health clinic for disabled, providing specialized therapy. Website launching Feb 2.
data.description: In Aberdeen, Maryland, Monarc LLC is set to launch its website, monarcmd.org, on February 2nd, 2026, showcasing a unique approach. This nonprofit will offer specialized mental health therapy directly to individuals with disabilities. This innovative model will be implemented at The Arc NCR.

data.title: BGL acquires Reclamation Technologies USA and Tradewater LLC to establish new environmental services platform. #EnvironmentalServices #Acquisition
data.description: On January 30, 2026, in Cleveland, a noteworthy announcement was made by Brown Gibbons Lang & Company (BGL), a prominent investment bank and financial advisory firm. They revealed that Reclamation Technologies USA (RTI), a top provider of refrigerant recovery and reclamation services, has partnered with them. RTI specializes in delivering comprehensive services in this sector, marking a significant move for both companies. This partnership is anticipated to bring about advancements in the refrigerant recovery industry, with RTI being a key player in the market.

data.title: Trump's Fed Chair pick leads to Gold, Silver's worst day since 1980. Silver plunges 33% to $78/ounce. Gold falls to $4,700/ounce, marking 12% decline. Silver surging by 60% collapses to 10%. SPDR Gold Shares stock shows noticeable weakness.
data.description: Precious metals experienced a sharp selloff after President Trump announced Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve chairman, ending a recent rally. Silver prices plummeted by 33% to $78 an ounce, marking its worst single-day decline since 1980. Gold also saw a significant drop, falling to $4,700 per ounce, a 12% daily decline. The SPDR Gold Shares (GLD) stock showed weakness amid fears of the Fed's independence being compromised. Just the day before, silver had been on track for its best monthly performance since 1864, but this surge collapsed into a 10% monthly gain. This dramatic reversal in precious metal prices was attributed to concerns over Fed policies.
